libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: If tests exercise com.mchange.v2.naming.ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(Reference, Name, Context, Hashtable) or the overload with PropertiesConfig but without an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set, set the system property `com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist` to the concrete ObjectFactory class name used by the test before the call, and clear/restore it afterward. Alternatively, prefer the overload that accepts an explicit Set containing the test ObjectFactory class name. Keep the whitelist narrow; do not use broad/wildcard ObjectFactory acceptance unless the test specifically covers that security behavior.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact POM declares optional integrations such as Typesafe Config, Log4j, Log4j2, and SLF4J, but they are optional and not required for baseline meaningful coverage.
A global system property can leak between tests if it is not restored, causing order-dependent JVM or native test behavior.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 does not need Docker or extra non-test dependencies for meaningful utility-library coverage, but its JNDI ReferenceableUtils path now requires an ObjectFactory whitelist when using referenceToObject overloads that do not pass an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set.
